title: Death Verification Request Page with OTP, File Uploads, and Admin Dashboard Integration

requirements.text: Create a new frontend route '/death-verification-request' with a secure Death Verification Request page styled in a dark professional theme consistent with the existing app palette. The page must include: Full Name of Deceased User (text input), Registered Email of Deceased User (email input), Heir's Full Name (text input), Relationship to Deceased (dropdown or text input), Government ID Upload (required, PDF/JPG/PNG, max 10MB), Official Death Certificate Upload (required, PDF/JPG/PNG, max 10MB), and Optional Relationship Proof Upload (PDF/JPG/PNG, max 10MB). Display privacy and security callout labels (e.g., 'All data is encrypted and handled confidentially'). Register this route in App.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: Route '/death-verification-request' renders without errors
All six form fields are present and labeled clearly
File upload inputs accept only PDF, JPG, PNG and reject other formats with an inline error message
Files exceeding 10MB are rejected with an inline error message
Page uses the existing dark theme color palette
Privacy/security callout is visible on the page

requirements.text: Implement an in-app OTP verification step on the Death Verification Request page. Before the form can be submitted, the heir must request an OTP (generated in the frontend, displayed on screen with a copy-to-clipboard button, consistent with the existing OTP pattern in LoginPage.tsx and RegistrationPage.tsx). The heir must enter the correct OTP to unlock the final submit button. This step should be clearly labeled as 'Verify Your Identity before Submission'.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Send OTP' / 'Generate OTP' button is present on the page
OTP is displayed in-app with a copy-to-clipboard button after generation
The Submit button is disabled until the correct OTP is entered
Entering an incorrect OTP shows an error message
Entering the correct OTP enables the Submit button

requirements.text: After successful OTP verification and form submission, replace the form with a confirmation message: 'Your request has been received and is under verification. You will be notified within 3–5 business days.' Display this in a styled confirmation card with a checkmark icon and security badge.
acceptanceCriteria: On successful submission the form is hidden and the confirmation card is shown
The confirmation card displays the exact message specified
A success/checkmark icon and security badge are visible on the confirmation card

requirements.text: Add a backend Motoko data type and stable storage for DeathVerificationRequest records. Each record must store: deceasedFullName (Text), deceasedEmail (Text), heirFullName (Text), relationshipToDeceased (Text), governmentIdBlob (Blob), deathCertificateBlob (Blob), relationshipProofBlob (optional Blob), status (variant: #PendingVerification | #Approved | #Rejected), submittedAt (Int timestamp), and a unique requestId (Nat). Expose a public update function submitDeathVerificationRequest that accepts these fields, stores the record with status #PendingVerification, and returns the requestId. Add a timestamped activity log entry for each submission.
acceptanceCriteria: DeathVerificationRequest type is defined with all required fields
submitDeathVerificationRequest function is callable and stores the record
Newly created records have status #PendingVerification
A timestamped activity log entry is created on submission
The function returns a unique requestId

requirements.text: Expose a backend admin-only query function getDeathVerificationRequests that returns all DeathVerificationRequest records. Also expose an admin-only update function updateDeathVerificationStatus(requestId: Nat, newStatus: variant{#Approved; #Rejected}) that updates the status of a request and logs the action. Enforce admin-only access using the existing role-based access control pattern in main.mo.
acceptanceCriteria: getDeathVerificationRequests is accessible only to admin principals
updateDeathVerificationStatus updates the correct record's status
Non-admin calls to these functions return an authorization error
Status updates are recorded in the activity log

requirements.text: Add a 'Death Verification Requests' section to the existing Admin dashboard (AdminLegalVerificationPage.tsx or a new AdminDeathVerificationPage.tsx). Display a table of all submitted DeathVerificationRequest records showing: requestId, deceasedFullName, deceasedEmail, heirFullName, relationshipToDeceased, status badge (Pending Verification / Approved / Rejected), and submittedAt timestamp. Each row must have Approve and Reject action buttons that call updateDeathVerificationStatus. Use the existing SecurityBadge component for status display.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin dashboard shows the Death Verification Requests table
All required columns are displayed
Status is shown using SecurityBadge component
Approve and Reject buttons trigger status update and refresh the table
Non-admin users cannot access this section

requirements.text: Add a navigation link to the Death Verification Request page in the public-facing Navbar (visible to unauthenticated users) or on the LandingPage as a CTA, labeled 'Submit Heir Verification Request'. Register the route in App.tsx router configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: A link to '/death-verification-request' is accessible from the public navigation or landing page
Clicking the link navigates to the Death Verification Request page
The route is registered in App.tsx

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture)
Do not edit any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui
OTP must be displayed in-app (no email or SMS delivery — consistent with existing platform pattern)
File uploads must be stored as Blobs in the backend canister; no external cloud storage
Admin approval is mandatory — no auto-approval of death verification requests
File validation (PDF/JPG/PNG only, max 10MB) must be enforced on the frontend before submission
nonGoals: External email or SMS OTP delivery
Integration with government death registry APIs
AI document analysis or confidence scoring (separate future feature)
Automatic access grant without admin approval
